Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Aug-18-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: BECKY CACHIA-RIEDL, ESQ. FOR DEFENDANTS/MOVING PARTIES, HERIBERTO OCASIO, JR., M.D., ST. LUKE'S HOSPITAL

RULING:
TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendants' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery is granted in part and denied in part. The motion is granted as to Special Interrogatory No. 3. The motion is denied as to Special Interrogatory No. 4. Plaintiff's right to privacy outweigh the relevance of the interrogatory without good cause. The motion is denied as to Request for Production Nos. 1 and 2 as irrelevant. The motion is denied as to Request for Production Nos. 4-6 as the home and business phones are irrelevant. As to the cell phone records, the motion is moot. The motion is granted as to Request for Production Nos. 7. Plaintiff is ordered to respond further to declare that diligent search and reasonable inquiry have been made. The motion is denied as to Request for Production Nos. 8 and 9. The claim of privilege is sustained and the response is adequate. The motion is denied as to Request for Production Nos. 10 and 30 and 31 as irrelevant. The motion is denied as to Request for Production Nos. 11-16 as irrelevant. The motion is granted as to Request for Production No. 17 as the objections are overruled and ordered stricken. However, the response may be otherwise sufficient. The motion is denied as to Request for Production Nos. 18-29 and 32-42 as irrelevant. Further responses shall be made within 15 days of notice of this order. All requests for sanctions are denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (612/HEWLETT)
